name: _MSGInit@0
notes: clear the 0x22-slot message queue at 0x522d40 (stride 0xd5; +6 = fire-time, 0xffff = empty) and the hide-stack

name: _MSGSend@32
notes: THE radio/order primitive: queue a T_MSG {flags,rand,fromId,toId,fireT=_currentT+delay,param,kind,payload[<=200],len} for one or a broadcast set of recipients. Recipient 0x8000/8001/8002 fan out via WNGPart/WNGWingmen to leader/wing; 0x8003+thisComputer routes to a human. AI recipients get ImmediateService; non-local recipients are mirrored by MPMsgSend. If the message is TO the player and audible (radio not silenced, kind in the spoken set) it also calls SAYMsg. Kinds are the SayProc opcodes (attack/break/contact/splash/…)
type: undefined4 __stdcall MSGSend(undefined4, undefined4, undefined4, undefined4, undefined4, undefined4, undefined4, undefined4)

name: _MSGReceive@8
notes: pull the first due message (fireT<=_currentT) matching a recipient mask into the receive buffer at 0x522c60; SAYMsg it if audible; compacts the queue; 0x800b = any-AI wildcard

name: _MSGHide@0
notes: push the suppress-audio flag (0x522c58) and set it — used to bracket a burst of orders so only the net effect is spoken

name: _MSGPop@0
notes: pop the suppress-audio flag saved by MSGHide

name: _MSGMultiCanGet@0
notes: is there a free slot, and pick the oldest pending broadcast(0x400) message to expire, so the multiplayer relay always has room

name: MSGMultiPurge
notes: alternate entry / helper of MSGMultiCanGet: find a matching slot then expire the oldest 0x400 broadcast

name: @MSGMultiGet@4
notes: copy an incoming network message into a free queue slot; ImmediateService the recipient if it is a live local AI

name: _MSGRemoveCurObj@0
notes: expire every queued message addressed to _curId (object death cleanup)

name: @MSGRemoveSecondaries@4
notes: expire queued secondary (non-primary, +0x1d set) messages from a given sender

name: _SAYInit@0
notes: reset the SAY chatter cooldown + per-topic "last said" timestamps

name: _SAYInit2@0
notes: second-stage SAY reset: seed home/succeeded/failed flags from MISSIONSucceededForThisPlayer

name: _SAYShutdown@0
notes: SAY teardown (no-op)

name: @SAYMsg@4
notes: render one queued T_MSG: push its sender as cur-obj, then dispatch to the object class SayProc via CallUtilProc(6) (PLANESayProc etc.) or SAYDefaultSayProc

name: _SAYDefaultSayProc
notes: the fallback SayProc: emit the literal $-prefixed payload string, then FinishSayLine

name: SayLineReset
notes: clear the two SAY line accumulators (text at 0x552ff0, sound-name list at 0x553050)

name: SayLineAppend
notes: append a {text,sound} pair to the SAY line accumulators (comma-separating stacked sound names)

name: FinishSayLine
notes: finish a spoken line: prefix the speaker callsign (or "CO-PILOT"/"VOICE"), HUDMessage the text, play the queued sound names at radio volume, arm the chatter cooldown

name: SayScaleTime
notes: scale a chatter-cooldown duration by the current time compression

name: SayResetSoundHandle
notes: reset the looping-say sound handle (0x4ff058)

name: SayPlaySoundList
notes: play a comma-separated list of .5K sound files as one radio transmission (default extension .5K)

name: SayObjName
notes: format an object's spoken name: its assigned callsign, or "<colour> <n>" for a wing member, or the type name

name: PlaneSayBreak
notes: overlapping alternate entry into PLANESayProc case 5 (the "Break"/"Approach" direction line)

name: SayFatigueLow
notes: fatigue gate: is the speaker's fatigue byte (+1) below a threshold

name: SayPickLine
notes: choose one of N alternative lines for a topic — random when no sender, else the sender id modulo N so a given plane is consistent — and append it

name: SayContactType
notes: build the "N bandit(s)" contact-type phrase: MiG-17/19/21 special-cased by type-name prefix, else generic bandit + the aspect word chosen from the target facing bits

name: SayClock
notes: append a clock-position phrase ("your 3 o'clock", "high"/"low") from a bearing + elevation

name: SayNumberWord
notes: append a spelled number-word sound (from _numberSay) + its text

name: SayRange
notes: append a spoken range in miles ("2 miles" / "%d.MILE")

name: SaySpellNumber
notes: spell an integer digit-by-digit as _NUM sound files + text

name: SayWaypointCall
notes: build the "Proceed to waypoint N, bearing X, descend to angels Y" AWACS steer call

name: SayContactCall
notes: build the full "Contact, [multiple/pair of] bandits, <clock>, <range>, please advise" AWACS contact call, weather-gated via WRWeatherEffects

name: @SAYRearmMessage@8
notes: "Plane Re-Armed and Re-Fueled" radio line

name: SayFlushLine
notes: flush the accumulated SAY line to a network MSGSend(kind 0x24) so remote players hear it, then arm the cooldown

name: @SAYSuppRadarMessage@12
notes: AWACS/GCI supplemental-radar link on/off/broken radio lines (temporarily forces radio-on to guarantee the message is heard)

name: @SAYLowFuelMessage@8
notes: the fuel-state ladder: Joker/Bingo/"running on fumes"/"out of gas" lines, each fired once via plane-flags latch bits (0x50cfef 0x8000..0x400000)

name: @SAYTranslate@4
notes: when the player flies a MiG (side 0x14/0x15), swap an English distress .5K for its Russian equivalent (AARRRGH/EJECT variants); otherwise pass the filename through

name: _SAYFortAircraft
notes: HUD list of the fort mission's aircraft types + counts (Shift+I report)

name: _SAYFortStatus
notes: HUD list of each fort's % destroyed (Shift+F report)
